name: M.S. in Industrial Engineering

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: Only three courses are surfaced (IE 700B Master's Project, IE 701B Master's Thesis, IE 791 Graduate Seminar), all at depth 0, so the entire taught core of a 30-credit IE master's (optimization, stochastic models, engineering statistics, simulation, quality) is invisible and no coursework progression can be traced. The presence of both a project (IE 700B) and a thesis (IE 701B) track does signal a sensible thesis/non-thesis choice, but that is only the culminating layer of the degree.
dimensions.suggestion: Surface the required core and elective coursework (the department already offers IE 604 Advanced Engineering Statistics, IE 621 Systems Analysis and Simulation, IE 659 Supply Chain Engineering and IE 673 TQM) so a coherent foundations-to-research sequence is visible.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: deepestPrereqChain is 0 and missingCourseCount is 0, so the graph is clean but completely flat; the only three courses shown are research vehicles with no prerequisites, which is appropriate for a thesis but leaves no evidence of gating or sequencing across the degree.
dimensions.suggestion: Record prerequisites on the taught courses (e.g., an engineering-statistics prerequisite ahead of design-of-experiments and simulation electives) so a minimal but real prerequisite structure appears.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or educational objectives are present in the data, giving a prospective research student no stated competencies (optimization, applied statistics, systems modeling) to judge the degree against.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish 3-5 program learning outcomes describing the analytical and research capabilities an IE M.S. graduate should demonstrate.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: statedTotalCredits is 30, which is transparent and standard for an IE master's, but creditReconcileDelta is null and no plan of study is parsed, so the split among required core, electives, and project/thesis credits is not shown.
dimensions.suggestion: State how the 30 credits divide among core, electives, and the IE 700B/701B research requirement so the balance is transparent.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: The page is sparse: three courses, no plan of study, no labeled terms, and no core/elective grouping. The descriptions that do appear (IE 700B, IE 701B) are readable, but the entry cannot orient a prospective student to what the degree actually teaches.
dimensions.suggestion: Rebuild the catalog entry with the full course list, a plan of study, and a credit summary.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: With only project, thesis, and seminar vehicles visible, there is no taught content to assess for currency; the discipline is highly relevant, but the page demonstrates none of the analytics, operations-research, or data-driven methods that define contemporary IE practice.
dimensions.suggestion: Add and display modern electives (data analytics/machine learning for IE, healthcare systems, digital-twin simulation) so the degree's currency is demonstrable.

descriptionQuality.justification: Only three research-vehicle descriptions are present, and while they are clear (IE 700B/701B specify advisor approval and a written report/thesis), the sample is far too small to establish description quality across the degree.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: None of the three shown courses lists a prerequisite; entries are unambiguous and free of dangling references (missingCourseCount 0), but the total absence of prerequisites provides no evidence of intended sequencing.

missingTopics.rationale: No linear/integer programming or network-optimization course is visible, yet it is the analytical backbone of an IE master's.
Probabilistic modeling (Markov chains, queueing, reliability) is a core IE competency absent from the surfaced courses.

missingTopics.topic: Data analytics and machine learning for IE
missingTopics.rationale: Modern IE relies on predictive analytics and statistical learning; no such course appears on the page.
A traditional IE pillar (the department offers IE 661/664/665) is not represented in this program's visible list.

missingTopics.topic: Design of experiments and quality engineering
missingTopics.rationale: Six-sigma/DOE methods central to IE quality practice are not surfaced despite IE 604 existing in the department.

emergingTrends.rationale: IE is rapidly absorbing machine learning for forecasting, scheduling, and anomaly detection; the degree should surface an analytics course.
Cyber-physical modeling of production and service systems is now standard IE research; a simulation/digital-twin course (building on IE 621) would position the degree well.
Applying IE methods to hospital operations and care delivery is a fast-growing frontier well suited to an urban research university.
Real-time, analytics-based supply-chain management is a leading IE direction the program can foreground via IE 659.
As automation spreads, IE research increasingly studies human-automation teaming, extending the department's human-factors strength.

topSuggestions: Rebuild the catalog page to surface the required core and elective coursework, since three research-vehicle courses cannot represent a 30-credit degree.
Publish 3-5 program learning outcomes describing analytical and research competencies.
State the credit split among core, electives, and project/thesis so the 30-credit plan is transparent.
Add and display modern analytics/ML electives to demonstrate currency.
Record prerequisites on taught courses to show intended sequencing.
